event: UFC Fight Night: Song vs. Figueiredo

items.fight_news: Song's boxing relies heavily on his lead hook counter. Figueiredo tends to drop his right hand when throwing the low calf kick, creating a massive opening for Song to pivot and land clean on the jaw.

Figueiredo needs to establish the front headlock early to neutralize Song's forward pressure. If he can wrap the neck during Song's explosive blitzes, he can drain the Chinese fighter's cardiovascular reserves in the championship rounds.
Zhang's offensive combinations are lethal, but he consistently leaves his chin high on exits. Menifield has exceptional timing on the overhand right when opponents break the clinch.
If Zhang gets sloppy backing away from the pocket, Menifield will capitalize immediately. The tactical priority for Zhang is circling out laterally instead of retreating in straight lines.
Pavlovich has unparalleled reach and straight punching power. Teixeira is a massive heavyweight prospect and must utilize lateral movement to avoid the early blitz that Pavlovich is famous for.
The key metric here is Teixeira's defensive footwork against the cage: if he gets backed up onto the fence, Pavlovich's volume and power become an insurmountable mathematical problem.
Asakura's striking stance is heavily bladed, making him highly susceptible to lead leg attacks. Smotherman needs to chop the calf early to take away Asakura's explosive karate-style forward movement.
Asakura will counter this by trying to time right hands over the top of the low kicks. The fight hinges on who establishes their timing first in the opening two minutes.

Harris possesses a lethal anaconda choke and thrives in chaotic scrambles. Matthews has a solid wrestling base but must avoid shooting naked double legs, as Harris is elite at wrapping the neck in transition.
Keeping the fight standing and using technical, straight-punching boxing combinations is Matthews' safest and most statistically probable route to victory.
Perez relies heavily on inside leg kicks to set up his wrestling entries. Mudaerji operates from a long southpaw stance and counters beautifully with his straight left hand.
Perez must slip to the outside of the lead foot to avoid eating massive shots down the pipe while attempting to close the distance against the taller fighter.

bouts.title: Lee Yi-sak vs. Luis Felipe Dias

items.fight_news: Lee's judo throws are highly effective, but Dias is a high-level BJJ black belt who is incredibly dangerous off his back. Ground-and-pound comes with extreme risk here.
Lee must ensure his posture is perfect if he ends up in top control, or he risks falling directly into triangle and armbar traps set by the Brazilian.

bouts.title: Ding Meng vs. José Henrique Souza

items.fight_news: Ding pushes a relentless pace, relying on sheer output to overwhelm opponents. Souza has a massive height and reach advantage at welterweight and must use teep kicks to maintain distance.
If Souza's jab fails to keep Ding at bay, Ding will initiate dirty boxing exchanges where Souza's long levers become a severe disadvantage.
Aori's chin is legendary, but his defensive head movement is often lacking. Haddon has pinpoint accuracy and needs to focus on body-to-head combinations to break down Aori's guard.
If Haddon strictly head-hunts, he will likely gas out trying to finish an unfinishable fighter. Body work is the absolute tactical key for the Australian.
Tsuruya is a phenomenal grappler who chain-wrestles relentlessly from the opening bell. Gurule is stepping in on short notice and must focus entirely on sprawl-and-brawl tactics.
If Tsuruya locks his hands around Gurule's hips, the control time will heavily dictate the scorecards. Gurule's hip framing and underhooks are his only lifelines.
Xiong Jingnan brings ferocious boxing and incredible takedown defense to her Octagon debut. Hill must utilize her Muay Thai clinch and throw elbows on the break to disrupt Xiong's rhythm.
Standing at range and trading straight punches heavily favors Xiong's power mechanics. Hill must make the fight ugly inside the pocket.
Zhu uses excellent outside footwork to circle away from power. Vera needs to cut the cage off efficiently rather than mindlessly following Zhu around the perimeter.
The fighter who controls the center of the Octagon and forces the other to fight off the back foot will completely dictate the striking exchanges.
Loma's clinch game is the best in the strawweight division, but dragging Amorim to the mat is extremely dangerous. Amorim hunts leg locks aggressively from bottom positions.
Loma must use sweeping knees from the Thai plum and immediately disengage before the fight hits the ground.
